title: salix estipulata
intro: Salix estipulata, also known as the Mexican willow or coyote willow, is a species of willow tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Salix estipulata include reducing inflammation, soothing sore throats, and promoting wound healing. Salix estipulata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat various health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
